title: leptocarpus scoparius
side_effects: Leptocarpus scoparius can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to the plant's ability to irritate the digestive system. In severe cases, excessive consumption can lead to liver and kidney damage, as well as an increased risk of bleeding due to the plant's anticoagulant properties. Additionally, the plant's toxic compounds can cause allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in sensitive individuals. If ingested or applied topically in excessive amounts, Leptocarpus scoparius can cause severe side effects that may require medical attention.
